description: Logout or credential change leaves the old session/token valid, so a stolen or shared token keeps working.
body: Failure mode: logout clears only the client cookie, or a password reset does not revoke existing sessions/tokens, so a previously issued token remains valid until natural expiry. Detection signals: no server-side session deletion or token revocation on logout; password change keeps old refresh tokens alive; long access-token TTL with no revocation. Do not treat client-side cookie clearing as logout. Safer fallback: revoke the server session / refresh token on logout and on credential change, keep access tokens short, and add a test that a logged-out or reset token is rejected.
